name: Swamp cabbage / water spinach (Ipomoea aquatica) (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh (raw) leaves and stems

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Kjeldahl protein of raw water spinach.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Net available carbohydrate is therefore only ~1 g/100 g.

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: Not quantified by USDA; net non-fiber carbohydrate ~1 g/100 g, so free sugars are low (this is a low-sugar leafy green, not a sugar-rich fruit).

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Umar et al. 2007 (scialert doi:10.3923/jas.2007.803.809); Austin 2007 review (Econ Bot doi:10.1663/0013-0001(2007)61[123:WSIACA]2.0.CO;2)
components.evidence: Free glucose reported present in I. aquatica leaves; not individually quantified in the literature — included as present, unquantified.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Free fructose reported present; not individually quantified.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Sucrose reported present; not individually quantified.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Malakar & Choudhury 2015 (fresh Karamwa/I. aquatica leaves, oxalate 125 mg/100 g); Horizon e-Publishing PST oxalic-acid study (fresh 250 mg/100 g)
components.evidence: Dominant organic acid of water spinach; fresh-leaf values 125-250 mg/100 g (midpoint ~190). Reported as 125 mg with 125-250 range.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Austin 2007 review (Econ Bot); Umar et al. 2007
components.evidence: Malic acid reported present in I. aquatica leaves; not quantified in available sources — key omission from the FooDB profile.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Austin 2007 review (Econ Bot); Umar et al. 2007
components.evidence: Citric acid reported present; not quantified — key omission from the FooDB profile.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: USDA amino-acid panel (L-serine; plant serine is the L enantiomer — replaces the FooDB 'D-Serine' artifact).

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Protein-bound arginine present; specific USDA value not captured here — included as present.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Glutamic acid is the most abundant protein amino acid in leaf tissue; USDA value not captured — included as present.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Fresh-weight value (dry-weight literature values are ~5,458 mg/100 g DW).

components.name: iron
components.evidence: USDA mineral panel (single iron entry; speciation/compartment duplicates removed).

components.name: L-ascorbate
components.evidence: Vitamin C; the defining chemical compound of water spinach.

components.name: retinol/vitamin A
components.evidence: Vitamin A activity (~315 µg RAE), supplied as provitamin-A carotenoids, not preformed retinol (a plant food).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Principal provitamin-A carotenoid; source of the 6,300 IU vitamin A. Present, USDA µg value not captured here.

components.name: vitamin K (phylloquinone)
components.source: not quantified in USDA SR Legacy for this food
components.evidence: Green leafy vegetables are typically high in vitamin K, but USDA does not report a value for water spinach; left null to avoid fabrication.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Genuine plant osmolyte; assimilable methyl/N source. Present, unquantified.

components.name: quercetin
components.evidence: Genuine flavonol of I. aquatica; kept as real phytochemical.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate iron speciation entry; iron is already represented once. Redundant, not a distinct nutrient.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Compartmentalized model/BiGG artifact (subcellular localization), not a food-composition descriptor; duplicate of iron.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt ion — non-nutritive trace element for this food; FooDB/model noise, water spinach is not a cobalt source.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient of this vegetable.

remove.name: Iodine c
remove.reason: Compartment-tagged trace artifact; water spinach is not an iodine source.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Wrong stereochemistry — plant/protein serine is L-serine; D-serine is a FooDB prediction artifact. Replaced with L-serine.

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: Model/medium gas-exchange artifact, not a food nutrient.

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: Model/medium gas-exchange artifact, not a food nutrient.

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: Proton/charge-balance model artifact, not a food nutrient.

missing_added: oxalic acid (dominant organic acid, quantified 125-250 mg/100 g)
individual free sugars: D-glucose, D-fructose, sucrose
full quantitative USDA amino-acid profile (Trp, Thr, Ile, Leu, Lys, Met, Cys, Phe, Tyr, Val, His, Ala, Asp, Gly, Pro, Ser)
quantitative minerals (K, Na, Ca, Mg, P, Fe, Zn, Cu, Mn, Se)
quantitative vitamins (C, B1, B2, B3, B5, B6, folate, vitamin A)
total protein, total carbohydrate, dietary fiber, total fat, water
keep_uncovered: Glycolipids (genuine leaf membrane lipids; no simple BiGG met)
Pectic acid (cell-wall polysaccharide; no free-metabolite BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169301 (Waterspinach, raw; SR Legacy NDB 11482)
Umar, Ekwenye et al. 2007, Nutritional Composition of Water Spinach (Ipomoea aquatica) Leaves, J. Applied Sci. doi:10.3923/jas.2007.803.809
Austin D.F. 2007, Water Spinach (Ipomoea aquatica, Convolvulaceae): A Food Gone Wild, Economic Botany review
Malakar & Choudhury 2015, anti-nutritional factors of fresh Karamwa (Ipomoea aquatica) leaves (oxalate 125 mg/100 g fresh)
Horizon e-Publishing PST, comparative oxalic-acid levels in fresh/processed water spinach (fresh 250 mg/100 g)
Frontiers in Nutrition 2023, Chemical/biochemical characterization of Ipomoea aquatica, doi:10.3389/fnut.2023.1304903
notes: Water spinach is a low-sugar leafy green: with net non-fiber carbohydrate ~1 g/100 g, the dominant assimilable carbon/energy for a microbe is NOT free sugars but the free/protein amino acids (Asp, Glu, Ala, Leu, Lys, Thr, Val most abundant) and organic acids — chiefly oxalic acid (125-250 mg/100 g), with malic and citric acid also present (both absent from the original FooDB profile). Mineral/vitamin panel is USDA fresh-weight (FDC 169301); high in vitamin C (55 mg), provitamin-A carotenoids, K, Ca, Mg and Fe. Removed model/compartment artifacts (Fe3+/Fe2+ duplicates, CO2/O2/H+), trace non-nutrients (Ni, Co2+, iodine), and the implausible D-serine (replaced with L-serine).
